q: A startup wants to launch a web application globally without purchasing servers or paying upfront costs. Which cloud benefit enables this?
options: Paying a fixed monthly fee for reserved capacity
Trade capital expense for variable operational expense with pay-as-you-go pricing
Signing a 3-year contract for discounted server rates
Purchasing physical servers in each target region
explain: Cloud computing transforms CapEx (buying servers upfront) into OpEx (paying for what you use). This eliminates upfront investment and lets startups scale spending with actual usage.

q: A company's e-commerce site experiences 10x traffic during Black Friday. What cloud benefit allows them to handle this automatically?
options: High availability through multiple data centers
Economies of scale from shared infrastructure
Elasticity — automatically scaling resources up and down based on demand
Global reach through CDN distribution
explain: Elasticity is the ability to automatically provision and de-provision resources to match demand. AWS Auto Scaling adds capacity during Black Friday peaks and removes it after, paying only for what was used.

q: Which cloud deployment model keeps all infrastructure on-premises with no cloud connectivity?
options: Private cloud — dedicated infrastructure operated solely for one organization
Community cloud shared among organizations with similar requirements
Hybrid cloud combining on-premises and public cloud
Public cloud using shared AWS infrastructure
explain: Private cloud deploys dedicated infrastructure exclusively for one organization, either on-premises or in a dedicated hosted facility. All resources are isolated from other organizations.

q: A company uses both AWS for new workloads and keeps legacy apps on-premises. What deployment model is this?
options: Multi-cloud using multiple public cloud providers
Private cloud with on-premises resources only
Hybrid cloud combining on-premises and AWS cloud resources
Pure public cloud using only AWS services
explain: Hybrid cloud integrates on-premises infrastructure with public cloud services. AWS Direct Connect and VPN provide secure connectivity between the data center and AWS, enabling workload portability.

q: AWS operates data centers in multiple geographic locations worldwide. What is each distinct geographic location called?
options: Edge Location — CloudFront CDN point of presence
AWS Region — geographic area with multiple availability zones
Availability Zone — isolated data center within a region
Local Zone — AWS infrastructure extension near cities
explain: AWS Regions are distinct geographic areas (us-east-1, eu-west-1, etc.) each containing multiple Availability Zones. Choosing the right region affects latency, compliance, and disaster recovery.

q: A startup wants to launch a web application globally without purchasing servers or paying upfront costs. Which cloud benefit enables this?
options: Purchasing physical servers in each target region
Trade capital expense for variable operational expense with pay-as-you-go pricing
Signing a 3-year contract for discounted server rates
Paying a fixed monthly fee for reserved capacity
explain: Cloud computing transforms CapEx (buying servers upfront) into OpEx (paying for what you use). This eliminates upfront investment and lets startups scale spending with actual usage.

q: Which AWS concept describes the ability to acquire resources instantly and release them when no longer needed, with no human interaction?
options: Measured service tracking resource usage for billing
Broad network access through internet connectivity
On-demand self-service — provision resources without human intervention
Resource pooling sharing infrastructure among multiple customers
explain: On-demand self-service is a fundamental cloud characteristic: users can provision compute, storage, and networking resources instantly through a console or API without requiring human assistance from the provider.

q: A company benefits from AWS's massive scale which allows AWS to offer lower prices. What cloud benefit does this represent?
options: Massive economies of scale driving lower pay-as-you-go prices
Increased speed and agility launching resources in minutes
Elasticity scaling resources with demand
Global reach deploying in multiple regions
explain: Because AWS serves hundreds of thousands of customers, it achieves economies of scale that result in lower variable costs, which AWS passes on through lower prices than any single company could achieve on its own.

q: A developer can launch a new server on AWS in minutes instead of the weeks required to procure physical hardware. What cloud advantage is this?
options: Stop guessing capacity by scaling based on actual demand
Increased speed and agility — provision resources in minutes not months
Elasticity handling variable workloads efficiently
Economies of scale reducing infrastructure costs
explain: Cloud dramatically increases speed and agility: resources that took weeks to procure (purchase order, shipping, rack, configure) now take minutes via console or API, enabling rapid innovation and experimentation.

q: A company's e-commerce site experiences 10x traffic during Black Friday. What cloud benefit allows them to handle this automatically?
options: Global reach through CDN distribution
Economies of scale from shared infrastructure
Elasticity — automatically scaling resources up and down based on demand
High availability through multiple data centers
explain: Elasticity is the ability to automatically provision and de-provision resources to match demand. AWS Auto Scaling adds capacity during Black Friday peaks and removes it after, paying only for what was used.

q: Under the AWS shared responsibility model, which of the following is AWS responsible for?
options: Configuring security groups on EC2 instances
Managing IAM users and their permissions
Encrypting customer data stored in S3
Patching the hypervisor and physical host infrastructure
explain: AWS manages and patches the physical infrastructure, hypervisor, and managed service software. Customers manage their OS patches, application security, IAM, and network configuration.

q: Which AWS Support plan provides access to a Technical Account Manager (TAM) and is designed for enterprise customers with mission-critical workloads?
options: Business Support — 24/7 phone and chat support
Enterprise Support — TAM plus proactive guidance
Developer Support — business hours email support
Basic Support — free tier with documentation access
explain: Enterprise Support includes a dedicated Technical Account Manager (TAM) who provides proactive guidance, architectural reviews, and helps customers plan, deploy, and optimize their AWS environments.

q: A company wants to migrate its data center to AWS to eliminate the cost of managing physical hardware. What cloud benefit does this represent?
options: Go global in minutes by deploying in multiple regions
Trade variable expense for capital expense
Increase speed and agility through rapid resource provisioning
Stop spending money running and maintaining data centers
explain: Eliminating data center operations (power, cooling, physical security, hardware refresh cycles) is a core cloud benefit. Companies redirect that capital and staff effort toward innovation rather than infrastructure maintenance.

q: Which cloud deployment model keeps all infrastructure on-premises with no cloud connectivity?
options: Private cloud — dedicated infrastructure operated solely for one organization
Public cloud using shared AWS infrastructure
Community cloud shared among organizations with similar requirements
Hybrid cloud combining on-premises and public cloud
explain: Private cloud deploys dedicated infrastructure exclusively for one organization, either on-premises or in a dedicated hosted facility. All resources are isolated from other organizations.

q: Which AWS service provides a unified view of all AWS accounts and enables consolidated billing for an organization?
options: AWS Control Tower for account governance automation
AWS Config for compliance tracking across accounts
AWS IAM for identity management across accounts
AWS Organizations for multi-account management and consolidated billing
explain: AWS Organizations groups multiple AWS accounts under a master payer account, enabling consolidated billing (single invoice, volume discounts), service control policies, and centralized account governance.

q: A company wants to stop guessing how much infrastructure capacity they need. Which cloud benefit addresses this?
options: Go global in minutes using multiple AWS regions
Stop guessing capacity — scale up or down as actual needs change
Trade capital expense for variable expense with pay-as-you-go
Benefit from massive economies of scale reducing fixed costs
explain: With AWS, you provision based on actual demand instead of peak estimates. If you over-provision, you pay for idle capacity. Under-provision and you can't handle load. AWS Auto Scaling eliminates this guessing game.

q: A retail company operates stores across 50 countries and wants their customers to access content with low latency. Which AWS Global Infrastructure component enables this?
options: AWS Local Zones for enterprise applications
AWS Regions for processing data in customer locations
AWS Availability Zones for redundancy within each country
AWS Edge Locations for caching content globally near end users
explain: AWS Edge Locations (part of CloudFront) cache content globally much closer to end users than regions, providing low-latency content delivery. This reduces download times and improves user experience for geographically distributed audiences.

q: Which of the following is an example of a managed AWS service where AWS handles patching, scaling, and availability?
options: Amazon RDS — AWS manages database engine, backups, patching, and failover
EC2 with a self-managed Kubernetes cluster
EC2 instance where you install and manage your own database
An EC2-hosted application behind an Elastic Load Balancer
explain: Amazon RDS is a managed database service — AWS handles database patching, automated backups, Multi-AZ failover, and scaling. You manage database configuration and data, not the underlying infrastructure.

q: A startup is designing a healthcare platform and wants to understand cloud service models. Which service model provides complete control over the operating system?
options: PaaS — using managed deployment platforms
IaaS — using EC2 to manage OS, middleware, and application
SaaS — using a fully managed healthcare application
DaaS — using desktop-as-a-service
explain: IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) provides virtualized compute resources where the customer manages the OS, middleware, and application. EC2 is the classic example.

q: A company needs to choose between deploying in a single large EC2 instance vs many smaller ones. What AWS design principle do multiple smaller instances support?
options: Design for failure — assume any component can fail and architect to survive it
Performance efficiency using the most powerful instance types
Operational excellence through simplified management
Cost optimization through instance consolidation
explain: AWS Well-Architected Pillar: Reliability. Design for failure means assuming any single component will fail. Multiple smaller instances across AZs means no single failure takes down the application.

q: A company evaluates whether to migrate to AWS. On-premises requires $500,000 upfront hardware + $100,000/year operations. AWS costs $25,000/month. When does AWS become cost-effective?
options: After approximately 1.5 years when total costs favor AWS
After 7 years when cumulative costs are equivalent
Immediately, because monthly cost is less than annual
After 5 years when both reach equivalent cost
explain: TCO comparison: Year 1 on-premises = $600,000. Year 1 AWS = $300,000. Year 2 on-premises = $100,000. Year 2 AWS = $300,000. AWS becomes cheaper in Year 2 (~18 months).

q: Which pillar of the AWS Well-Architected Framework focuses on running and monitoring systems to deliver business value and continuously improve processes?
options: Security — protecting information and systems
Operational Excellence — running and improving operations effectively
Reliability — ensuring workload performs correctly consistently
Performance Efficiency — using resources efficiently
explain: Operational Excellence covers automation of operations, responding to events, and continuously improving processes and procedures. It includes performing operations as code and making small, reversible changes.

q: A company wants to evaluate their AWS architecture against best practices. Which free AWS tool provides this assessment?
options: AWS Well-Architected Tool for structured architectural review
AWS Trusted Advisor checking against best practices
AWS Inspector scanning for security vulnerabilities
AWS Config evaluating resource configuration compliance
explain: The AWS Well-Architected Tool provides a consistent process to review architectures against the six pillars, identify high-risk issues, and generate improvement plans through a series of questions.

q: A manufacturing company states 'We no longer maintain our own data centers.' Which cloud benefit is this describing?
options: High availability through global infrastructure and redundancy
Agility through infrastructure automation and rapid deployment
Elasticity to scale resources automatically based on demand
Economies of scale from AWS managing resources for thousands of customers
explain: Economies of scale is where AWS's massive infrastructure serving millions of customers drives down per-unit costs through shared resources and efficiency individual companies cannot achieve.

q: What distinguishes a 'serverless' architecture from a traditional server-based one?
options: Serverless is only suitable for small applications under 100 users
Serverless requires more configuration than traditional server management
Serverless uses no computing resources at all
Serverless means you don't provision or manage servers — the cloud provider handles scaling and availability automatically
explain: Serverless (Lambda, DynamoDB, S3) means you write code or use services without provisioning or managing servers. AWS handles scaling, patching, and availability automatically. You pay only per invocation/usage.

q: What is a key characteristic of cloud computing related to how multiple customers use the same physical infrastructure?
options: Measured service billing based on actual consumption
On-demand self-service with instant provisioning
Rapid elasticity scaling resources up and down on demand
Resource pooling serving multiple customers from shared physical resources with multi-tenancy
explain: Resource pooling is a core cloud characteristic where the provider serves multiple customers from a shared pool of physical resources, with customers getting virtual resources from a common infrastructure, achieving economies of scale.

q: Which AWS program helps customers migrate workloads to AWS by providing credits, training, and technical resources?
options: AWS Migration Acceleration Program (MAP) for enterprise migrations
AWS Partner Network for technology partner solutions
AWS Marketplace for purchasing third-party software
AWS Free Tier for experimenting with AWS services
explain: The AWS Migration Acceleration Program (MAP) provides financial, operational, and technical support including migration credits, training, and AWS Solutions Architect engagement for large-scale enterprise migrations.

q: What is the difference between 'vertical scaling' and 'horizontal scaling' on AWS?
options: Vertical scaling increases the size/power of one instance; horizontal scaling adds more instances
They are identical approaches with different AWS service names
Vertical scaling is for databases; horizontal scaling is for web servers only
Vertical scaling adds more instances; horizontal adds more capacity to one instance
explain: Vertical scaling (scale up) increases instance size (t3.medium → m5.large). Horizontal scaling (scale out) adds more instances behind a load balancer. Horizontal is preferred for cloud-native, fault-tolerant architectures.

q: A company wants to test a new idea by spinning up resources for 2 hours, testing, then shutting down with minimal cost. What cloud benefit supports this?
options: Ability to experiment quickly and cheaply — provision, test, then de-provision paying only for 2 hours
Global reach for deploying tests in multiple regions
Economies of scale reducing long-term infrastructure costs
High availability ensuring continuous uptime during testing
explain: Cloud enables cheap experimentation — try new ideas with minimal cost and risk. Failed experiments can be shut down immediately. This accelerates innovation cycles compared to waiting weeks and spending thousands on physical hardware.

q: An e-commerce business uses AWS but maintains legacy systems on-premises for compliance. What cloud deployment model is this?
options: Private cloud with AWS-managed dedicated resources
Hybrid cloud combining on-premises infrastructure with AWS public cloud
Public cloud with additional public cloud services
Multi-cloud using AWS and other cloud providers
explain: Hybrid cloud combines on-premises infrastructure with public cloud resources (like AWS), allowing organizations to maintain legacy systems while leveraging cloud benefits.

q: Which AWS service provides a global network of content delivery that caches content at edge locations near end users?
options: Amazon Route 53 for global DNS routing
Amazon CloudFront CDN for caching content at edge locations
Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration for fast uploads
AWS Global Accelerator for network performance optimization
explain: Amazon CloudFront is a CDN with 400+ edge locations globally. It caches static and dynamic content near users, reducing latency and offloading origin servers. It integrates with S3, EC2, and custom origins.

q: A startup reviews the AWS Well-Architected Framework. What are the main architectural pillars?
options: Cost, Security, Speed, and Compliance
AWS services, features, pricing, and support
Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability
Availability, Scalability, Durability, and Redundancy
explain: The AWS Well-Architected Framework consists of 6 pillars: Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, and Sustainability. These guide cloud architecture decisions.

q: AWS has data centers across the globe. What is the benefit of this global infrastructure for customers?
options: Customers can deploy applications globally in minutes to reduce latency for users worldwide
All data is automatically replicated across all regions
Global infrastructure eliminates the need for disaster recovery planning
All AWS services are available in every region simultaneously
explain: AWS's global infrastructure of 30+ regions enables companies to deploy applications close to users worldwide in minutes, reducing latency. Previously, global expansion required years of physical infrastructure setup.

q: A company wants to control which AWS services their developers can use across all accounts in their organization. Which service enforces this?
options: AWS Config rules monitoring service usage compliance
AWS CloudTrail logging all service API calls
IAM policies attached to individual user accounts
AWS Organizations Service Control Policies (SCPs) restricting services at the organizational level
explain: Service Control Policies (SCPs) in AWS Organizations set maximum permission guardrails across all accounts in an OU or organization. Even account admins can't exceed SCP boundaries, enabling centralized governance.

q: Which AWS service allows you to create and manage encryption keys used to protect your data, with full audit trails?
options: AWS Secrets Manager for storing application credentials
AWS Certificate Manager for SSL/TLS certificates
AWS CloudHSM for dedicated hardware security modules
AWS Key Management Service (KMS) for creating and managing cryptographic keys
explain: AWS KMS creates and manages Customer Master Keys (CMKs) for encrypting data across AWS services. It provides centralized key management, automatic rotation, and full CloudTrail audit logs of key usage.

q: A company needs to store database passwords and API keys securely with automatic rotation. Which AWS service is purpose-built for this?
options: Amazon S3 with server-side encryption for secret files
AWS KMS storing encryption keys
AWS Secrets Manager for securely storing and rotating credentials
AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store for configuration data
explain: AWS Secrets Manager stores, manages, and automatically rotates secrets like database passwords, API keys, and OAuth tokens. It integrates with RDS, Redshift, and DocumentDB for native credential rotation.

q: What is multi-factor authentication (MFA) and why is it critical for AWS root accounts?
options: An additional authentication factor beyond password, critical for root accounts to prevent account takeover
A backup authentication method used only when passwords expire
A compliance requirement only for regulated industries
An optional feature only needed for production accounts
explain: MFA requires a second factor (TOTP app, hardware key) beyond the password. AWS root accounts have unrestricted access to all resources — if compromised, attackers can delete everything. MFA is mandatory best practice for root.

q: Which AWS service monitors for malicious activity and unauthorized behavior across your AWS account using ML and threat intelligence?
options: Amazon Inspector scanning for vulnerabilities
Amazon GuardDuty detecting threats using ML and threat intelligence
AWS Config tracking configuration changes
AWS Security Hub aggregating security findings
explain: Amazon GuardDuty analyzes CloudTrail logs, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs using ML and threat intelligence to detect threats like credential compromise, unusual API calls, and crypto mining without requiring agents.

q: A company needs to assess their EC2 instances for known vulnerabilities and unintended network exposure. Which service automates this?
options: AWS Security Hub for compliance dashboard
Amazon Inspector for automated vulnerability assessment of EC2 and containers
AWS Config for configuration compliance checking
Amazon GuardDuty for threat detection
explain: Amazon Inspector automatically scans EC2 instances and container images for software vulnerabilities (CVEs) and unintended network exposure, generating prioritized findings with remediation recommendations.

q: For an EC2 instance, what does the shared responsibility model define regarding management?
options: AWS manages only the physical servers; customer manages everything above
AWS manages everything including applications and data
Customer manages the infrastructure; AWS provides billing and support
AWS manages infrastructure; customer manages OS, applications, and data
explain: The shared responsibility model divides duties: AWS handles hardware, virtualization, networking. Customer handles OS, application software, data, and security configurations.

q: A developer accidentally commits AWS access keys to a public GitHub repository. What should be done immediately?
options: Deactivate and delete the exposed access keys immediately, then create new ones
Change the IAM user's password to prevent unauthorized access
Enable MFA on the account and wait 24 hours for the keys to expire
Contact GitHub to remove the committed keys from history
explain: Exposed access keys must be immediately deactivated/deleted in IAM — they can be used within minutes of exposure. Check CloudTrail for unauthorized usage, then create new keys and update applications.

q: Which AWS service provides a centralized security dashboard aggregating findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, and other security services?
options: AWS Security Hub for centralized security posture management
Amazon CloudWatch for operational metrics and alarms
Amazon GuardDuty for threat detection aggregation
AWS Config for compliance and configuration tracking
explain: AWS Security Hub aggregates security findings from multiple AWS services and partner tools into a single dashboard, normalizes findings using ASFF format, and provides automated compliance checks (CIS, PCI, HIPAA).

q: A healthcare provider needs 99.99% uptime for patient records. What cloud architecture characteristic enables this?
options: Elasticity to scale capacity during demand peaks
Global reach to serve patients worldwide
Economies of scale reducing operational costs
High availability through redundancy across multiple Availability Zones
explain: High availability uses redundancy across multiple Availability Zones and automated failover to ensure systems continue operating even during component failures. This meets stringent uptime requirements.

q: Which AWS service enables compliance with regulations by recording all API calls made within your AWS account?
options: Amazon CloudWatch for performance monitoring and alerting
AWS CloudTrail logging all API calls for audit and compliance
Amazon GuardDuty detecting suspicious API usage patterns
AWS Config tracking resource configuration changes over time
explain: AWS CloudTrail logs every API call made in your AWS account including who made it, what was done, and when. This audit trail is essential for security investigations, compliance audits, and detecting unauthorized actions.

q: A company must comply with PCI DSS for credit card data. Which AWS program provides compliance reports and certifications?
options: AWS Artifact providing on-demand access to AWS compliance reports and certifications
AWS Security Hub running PCI DSS compliance checks
AWS Config rules for PCI DSS configuration compliance
AWS Trusted Advisor checking security best practices
explain: AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS security and compliance reports (SOC 2, PCI DSS, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA) through a self-service portal, enabling customers to demonstrate AWS's compliance posture to auditors.

q: A developer designs microservices where services communicate asynchronously. Why is this 'loosely coupled' architecture beneficial?
options: It automatically encrypts all communication
It reduces the total amount of code required
It eliminates the need for databases
Services can fail independently without cascading failures and scale independently
explain: Loose coupling means services don't depend directly on each other. One service can fail or be updated without affecting others. This improves resilience and flexibility — key cloud-native benefits.

q: Which AWS service protects web applications from common exploits like SQL injection and cross-site scripting (XSS)?
options: Amazon GuardDuty detecting web-based threats
Amazon Inspector scanning web applications for vulnerabilities
AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall) filtering malicious web traffic
AWS Shield protecting against DDoS attacks
explain: AWS WAF filters HTTP/HTTPS traffic using configurable rules, blocking common web attacks (OWASP Top 10 including SQL injection, XSS), bad bots, and rate-based rules. It integrates with CloudFront, ALB, and API Gateway.

q: A company wants to protect against large-scale DDoS attacks at the network and transport layers automatically. Which AWS service provides this for free?
options: AWS Firewall Manager managing WAF rules centrally
AWS WAF filtering application-layer attacks
Amazon GuardDuty detecting volumetric attack patterns
AWS Shield Standard protecting all AWS customers automatically at no charge
explain: AWS Shield Standard is automatically included for all AWS customers at no cost. It provides always-on protection against common network layer (Layer 3/4) DDoS attacks on EC2, ELB, CloudFront, and Route 53.

q: A DevOps team's application continues functioning when a database node fails by using replicas. What is this capability?
options: Failover — automatically switching to backup infrastructure
High availability — the system continues operating despite component failures
Redundancy — having duplicate components
Disaster recovery — restoring the entire system from backups
explain: Fault tolerance (closely related to high availability) is the system's ability to continue operating and serving requests even when individual components fail. Redundant database replicas exemplify this.

q: An organization reviews AWS's principle of least privilege for security. What does this mean?
options: Give all users full administrative access for simplicity
Allow everyone to access resources but log all activity
Grant users only the minimum permissions necessary for their jobs
Limit access only to AWS employees
explain: Least privilege means granting only the specific permissions required for a user or service to perform their job — nothing more. This minimizes the blast radius if credentials are compromised.

q: A company wants to detect if sensitive data like credit card numbers is stored in their S3 buckets without their knowledge. Which service helps?
options: Amazon Inspector scanning S3 objects for vulnerabilities
Amazon Macie using ML to discover and protect sensitive data in S3
Amazon GuardDuty monitoring S3 access patterns
AWS Config checking S3 bucket configuration compliance
explain: Amazon Macie uses ML to automatically discover, classify, and protect sensitive data (PII, financial data, credentials) in S3, providing visibility into what sensitive data you have and where it's stored.

q: Which AWS compliance program specifically addresses healthcare data protection requirements in the United States?
options: SOC 2 Type II for service organization controls
PCI DSS for payment card industry compliance
HIPAA — AWS signs Business Associate Agreements for covered healthcare workloads
ISO 27001 for information security management
explain: HIPAA (Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act) governs US healthcare data. AWS signs Business Associate Agreements (BAAs) and provides HIPAA-eligible services with appropriate safeguards for PHI.

q: A CFO asks about vendor lock-in with AWS. Which factor best addresses this concern?
options: Vendor lock-in is impossible; all clouds are identical
Customers can export data using standard formats and migrate to other providers
AWS owns all customer data and can hold it hostage
AWS legally guarantees zero switching costs
explain: While some AWS services are proprietary, customers can export data in standard formats. Migration has switching costs, but isn't technically impossible. It's a trade-off between cloud benefits and flexibility.

q: A company wants to centrally manage firewall rules across multiple AWS accounts and VPCs. Which service provides this?
options: AWS Firewall Manager centrally managing security policies across accounts
Amazon Inspector centrally managing vulnerability scanning
AWS WAF managing web application firewall rules per account
AWS Organizations SCPs restricting security service usage
explain: AWS Firewall Manager centrally configures and manages WAF rules, AWS Shield Advanced, Security Groups, and Network Firewall policies across multiple accounts in AWS Organizations from a single place.

q: A European company with GDPR data requirements wants to deploy their application. Where should they use AWS?
options: Use AWS US regions and hope GDPR doesn't apply
Deploy in AWS Europe (London, Frankfurt, Stockholm) regions to keep data in Europe
Use AWS global content delivery to bypass regional requirements
GDPR compliance is not relevant to AWS infrastructure
explain: AWS Regions are geographically separate locations. To comply with GDPR and data residency requirements, applications and data must be deployed in specific regions (eu-west-1, eu-central-1, etc.).

q: Which AWS service provides single sign-on (SSO) access to multiple AWS accounts and business applications?
options: AWS IAM for identity management across AWS accounts
AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO) for unified access to AWS accounts and apps
Amazon Cognito for customer-facing application authentication
AWS Directory Service for Active Directory integration
explain: AWS IAM Identity Center (formerly AWS SSO) provides centralized SSO to multiple AWS accounts and SAML 2.0 applications (Salesforce, Office 365) using a unified portal, integrating with Active Directory.

q: A company stores all infrastructure configuration in code (Terraform, CloudFormation). How does this support security?
options: Infrastructure as Code reduces security risks by eliminating manual console access
IaC enables consistent security configurations, version control, and peer review of infrastructure changes
IaC eliminates the need for security testing of infrastructure
IaC automatically encrypts all resources defined in the templates
explain: Infrastructure as Code (IaC) improves security by enabling peer review of changes, enforcing consistent security configurations, detecting drift, and providing audit history of all infrastructure modifications.

q: A media company designing a video platform requires compute, storage, and database. Which service model provides infrastructure control with managed services?
options: Pure SaaS — using only fully managed third-party applications
IaaS — using EC2 and S3 with self-managed databases
PaaS — using RDS, S3, and serverless for less operations
DaaS — using desktop-as-a-service
explain: PaaS balances control and operations: managed RDS, S3, and Lambda provide cloud benefits without heavy operations overhead. Ideal for teams wanting cloud benefits without server management.

q: Which AWS service provides dedicated, single-tenant hardware security modules (HSMs) for generating and using your own encryption keys?
options: AWS Certificate Manager for SSL/TLS certificate management
AWS Secrets Manager for application credential storage
AWS CloudHSM with dedicated single-tenant HSM hardware
AWS KMS using shared multi-tenant hardware
explain: AWS CloudHSM provides dedicated HSM hardware exclusively for your use. Unlike shared KMS, CloudHSM meets compliance requirements (FIPS 140-2 Level 3) where keys must never leave a dedicated hardware device.

q: A company receives an AWS abuse notification that their EC2 instance is sending spam emails. What should they do first?
options: Change the security group to block all outbound traffic and continue operations
Immediately investigate the instance for compromise, isolate it, and respond to AWS abuse team
Terminate the instance immediately and file an insurance claim
Ignore it since AWS may have made an error in detection
explain: AWS abuse notifications should be taken seriously immediately. Isolate the instance (modify security group, snapshot), investigate for compromise (check processes, logs), remediate, and respond to abuse@amazonaws.com within 24 hours.

q: What is the AWS Shared Responsibility Model's customer responsibility for Amazon RDS?
options: Ensuring RDS Multi-AZ failover works correctly
Patching the database engine software and OS
Maintaining physical hardware and hypervisor for RDS instances
Managing database schema, data encryption, and access control (IAM, security groups)
explain: For RDS, AWS manages OS patching, database engine patching, hardware, and Multi-AZ failover. Customers are responsible for database-level security (user accounts, permissions), data encryption settings, and security group configuration.

q: Which of the following is a customer's responsibility under the shared responsibility model for Amazon EC2?
options: Maintaining the physical host servers and network hardware
Patching the operating system, managing applications, and configuring security groups
Providing power, cooling, and physical security for the data center
Ensuring the hypervisor is secure and properly isolated between instances
explain: For EC2 (IaaS), customers own the guest OS — they must patch it, secure the applications, manage IAM credentials, configure security groups, and encrypt their data. AWS owns everything below the hypervisor.

q: A company wants to encrypt data in transit between their application and an RDS database. What should they enable?
options: S3 server-side encryption for data stored in the database
VPC peering to prevent traffic from traveling over the public internet
AWS KMS encryption keys for the RDS volume
SSL/TLS encryption for connections between the application and RDS endpoint
explain: Encryption in transit uses SSL/TLS to encrypt data as it travels between the application and RDS. RDS provides TLS certificates; applications must enable SSL in their connection string.

q: An architect explains: 'We pay for compute resources by the second when running.' Which cloud pricing benefit is this?
options: Reserved pricing forcing long-term commitments
Consolidated billing discounts
Pay-as-you-go model charging only for consumed resources
Spot instances for always-on workloads
explain: Pay-as-you-go pricing charges only for resources actively used. This is the fundamental cloud cost model enabling scalability without upfront investment.

q: Which AWS service provides a managed, scalable solution for DDoS protection with 24/7 access to the DDoS Response Team?
options: AWS Shield Advanced for enhanced DDoS protection with DRT access
AWS Shield Standard included automatically for all customers
Amazon CloudFront absorbing volumetric DDoS traffic at edge
AWS WAF filtering application-layer DDoS attacks
explain: AWS Shield Advanced provides enhanced DDoS detection, near real-time visibility, cost protection for DDoS-related scaling charges, and 24/7 access to the DDoS Response Team (DRT) for incident support.

q: A company wants to ensure only their own encryption keys protect their S3 data, with AWS never having access to the plaintext keys. What option achieves this?
options: SSE-KMS using Customer Managed Keys in AWS KMS
SSE-C — customer-provided encryption keys where the customer controls and stores keys outside AWS
SSE-S3 using AWS-managed keys automatically
S3 Object Lock preventing key access to AWS
explain: SSE-C (Server-Side Encryption with Customer-Provided Keys) allows customers to provide their own encryption keys with each request. AWS performs the encryption but never stores the key, providing maximum key control.

q: What AWS feature allows you to define and enforce which regions AWS resources can be created in?
options: AWS Organizations SCPs with region restriction conditions
IAM policies with region conditions restricting service access
Both IAM policies and SCPs can enforce region restrictions
AWS Config rules monitoring resource regions
explain: Region restrictions can be enforced both through IAM policies (Condition: aws:RequestedRegion) and SCP conditions. SCPs provide organization-wide enforcement that individual account admins cannot override.

q: A nonprofit mentions: 'We focus on our mission instead of managing servers.' Which benefit are they describing?
options: Elasticity to handle traffic spikes
Agility — offloading infrastructure management to AWS
Global reach across regions
High availability through redundancy
explain: Agility is offloading infrastructure complexity (servers, networking, updates, patches) to AWS so teams focus on applications and business logic.

q: Which AWS service integrates with Microsoft Active Directory to provide directory services in the AWS cloud?
options: AWS IAM Identity Center replacing Active Directory
AWS Directory Service providing managed Active Directory in AWS
AWS Organizations for multi-account directory management
Amazon Cognito for enterprise directory integration
explain: AWS Directory Service offers managed Microsoft Active Directory (AWS Managed Microsoft AD), Simple AD, and AD Connector for connecting to on-premises AD. It enables EC2 instances to join a domain and use AD authentication.

q: A company needs to meet GDPR requirements for data stored in AWS. What is their primary responsibility?
options: The customer is responsible for data classification, access controls, processing purposes, and data subject rights management
GDPR only applies to European AWS regions, not global deployments
AWS Artifact provides GDPR compliance that covers customer data automatically
AWS is fully responsible for GDPR compliance for all customer data
explain: GDPR compliance is a shared responsibility. AWS provides compliant infrastructure (certified data centers, data processing agreements). Customers are responsible for their data — what they collect, how they process it, and honoring data subject rights.

q: Comparing cloud deployment models, which is shared by multiple organizations but not the public?
options: Community cloud — shared by organizations with similar requirements
Private cloud — single organization only
Public cloud — shared by all
Hybrid cloud — on-premises plus public cloud
explain: Community cloud is shared among organizations with similar requirements (same industry, compliance needs) but not open to the general public. It's between private and public models.

q: A company needs a scalable object storage solution for storing millions of images with 99.999999999% durability. Which service should they use?
options: Amazon EFS elastic file system for shared storage
Amazon EBS block storage attached to EC2 instances
Amazon S3 object storage with eleven 9s of durability
Amazon Glacier for long-term archival storage
explain: Amazon S3 provides 99.999999999% (11 nines) of durability by redundantly storing data across multiple AZs. It's designed for unlimited scalability and is the standard for object storage in AWS.

q: A company wants to run a web server without managing the operating system, patching, or scaling. Which AWS service best fits?
options: Amazon Lightsail for simple VPS management
AWS Elastic Beanstalk deploying applications without managing underlying infrastructure
Amazon EC2 with auto-managed OS patching
Amazon ECS running containers on managed EC2 clusters
explain: AWS Elastic Beanstalk is a PaaS that automatically handles deployment, capacity provisioning, load balancing, auto-scaling, and health monitoring. You upload code and Beanstalk manages the infrastructure.

q: Which EC2 pricing model offers up to 90% discount for fault-tolerant workloads that can tolerate interruption?
options: On-Demand Instances with per-second billing
Dedicated Hosts for compliance and licensing requirements
Spot Instances using spare EC2 capacity at discounted prices
Reserved Instances with 1 or 3-year commitment
explain: EC2 Spot Instances use spare AWS capacity at up to 90% discount. AWS can reclaim Spot Instances with a 2-minute warning. Ideal for batch processing, data analysis, and fault-tolerant workloads that can checkpoint and resume.

q: A company runs a database that requires consistent compute performance 24/7 for the next 3 years. Which pricing model provides the lowest cost?
options: On-Demand Instances for maximum flexibility
Spot Instances for maximum discount
Reserved Instances with 3-year All Upfront payment
Savings Plans with compute flexibility
explain: Reserved Instances with a 3-year all-upfront commitment provide the maximum discount (up to 72% off On-Demand) for steady-state workloads with predictable usage. All-upfront maximizes savings vs monthly or partial upfront.

q: Which AWS service provides managed relational databases with automated backups, Multi-AZ failover, and no OS management?
options: Amazon RDS for managed relational databases (MySQL, PostgreSQL, etc.)
Amazon Aurora for cloud-native relational databases only
Amazon Redshift for data warehousing
Amazon DynamoDB for NoSQL database management
explain: Amazon RDS manages relational database engines (MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, Aurora) with automated backups, point-in-time recovery, Multi-AZ high availability, and automated patching.

q: A startup needs a NoSQL database that can handle millions of requests per second with single-digit millisecond latency. Which service is best?
options: Amazon Neptune for graph database workloads
Amazon RDS MySQL with read replicas for scaling
Amazon ElastiCache Redis for in-memory caching
Amazon DynamoDB fully managed NoSQL with consistent single-digit ms performance
explain: Amazon DynamoDB is a fully managed serverless NoSQL database delivering consistent single-digit millisecond performance at any scale, from zero to millions of requests per second with no capacity planning required.

q: A company reviews AWS Availability Zones. What is the primary purpose of AZs in AWS regions?
options: AZs replace the need for backups
Each region has only one AZ
AZs are geographically distant to serve customers worldwide
AZs are separate data centers within a region enabling high availability and disaster recovery
explain: Availability Zones are separate, isolated data centers within each AWS region. Deploying across multiple AZs provides high availability: if one AZ fails, others continue operating.

q: A company needs to distribute HTTP/HTTPS traffic across multiple EC2 instances with health checks and SSL termination. Which service provides this?
options: Classic Load Balancer for legacy EC2-Classic workloads
Network Load Balancer (NLB) for ultra-low latency TCP/UDP
Amazon Route 53 for DNS-based load balancing
Application Load Balancer (ALB) for Layer 7 HTTP/HTTPS routing
explain: ALB operates at Layer 7 (HTTP/HTTPS), providing path-based and host-based routing, SSL termination, sticky sessions, and WebSocket support. It integrates with EC2, ECS, Lambda, and supports content-based routing.

q: A company discusses cloud benefits and mentions 'infrastructure scales automatically during peak usage.' Which benefit is this?
options: Global reach through multiple regions
Cost optimization through reserved capacity
Economies of scale from bulk purchasing
Elasticity — automatically scaling resources based on demand
explain: Elasticity is the ability to automatically scale capacity up during traffic peaks and down during normal periods, ensuring you pay only for what you use.

q: Which S3 storage class is designed for infrequently accessed data that still needs millisecond retrieval, at lower cost than S3 Standard?
options: S3 Standard for frequently accessed data
S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval for archival with 1-12 hour retrieval
S3 Standard-IA (Infrequent Access) for less frequent access with instant retrieval
S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moving data between tiers
explain: S3 Standard-IA charges lower storage prices than S3 Standard but adds a per-GB retrieval fee. It's ideal for backups and disaster recovery data accessed monthly, with the same durability and instant retrieval as Standard.

q: A company wants to run containerized applications without managing EC2 instances or clusters. Which service provides serverless containers?
options: Amazon EKS managing Kubernetes control plane only
Amazon ECS on EC2 launch type managing your own cluster
AWS Fargate running containers without managing underlying servers
AWS Batch for containerized batch processing jobs
explain: AWS Fargate is a serverless compute engine for containers that removes the need to provision or manage EC2 instances. You define CPU/memory requirements and Fargate handles the infrastructure, scaling, and availability.

q: Which AWS service provides a petabyte-scale data warehouse for running complex analytical queries?
options: Amazon Redshift for petabyte-scale data warehousing and analytics
Amazon Aurora for fast cloud-native database queries
Amazon RDS for transactional relational database queries
Amazon DynamoDB for NoSQL analytical workloads
explain: Amazon Redshift is a fully managed data warehouse that can query petabytes of structured and semi-structured data using standard SQL. It uses columnar storage and MPP (massively parallel processing) for fast analytical queries.

q: Reviewing cloud service models, which provides the most control to the customer?
options: SaaS — no control, fully managed applications
IaaS — maximum control; customer manages OS, middleware, and applications
PaaS — some control over applications and data
Managed services — AWS manages everything
explain: IaaS provides maximum customer control: EC2 lets you manage the OS, runtime, middleware, and applications. PaaS and SaaS provide progressively less control but less operations work.

q: A company wants to run their application code without any server management and pay only when their code executes. This describes which model?
options: CaaS — Containers as a Service on managed Kubernetes
IaaS — Infrastructure as a Service with managed VMs
PaaS — Platform as a Service with managed runtime
Serverless computing using Lambda for event-driven execution
explain: Serverless computing (Lambda) runs code only when triggered by events, scales automatically from zero to massive scale, and charges per invocation + duration. No servers, no patches, no capacity planning required.

q: Which AWS service provides a fully managed message queue for decoupling application components?
options: Amazon SNS for publish/subscribe notifications
Amazon EventBridge for event-driven architecture routing
Amazon SQS for reliable message queuing between services
Amazon Kinesis for real-time streaming data processing
explain: Amazon SQS provides managed message queues for decoupling producers from consumers. Messages are stored durably until processed. SQS enables microservices to communicate without direct dependencies.

q: A company wants to send notifications to millions of subscribers across email, SMS, and mobile push simultaneously. Which service enables this?
options: Amazon SNS for pub/sub notifications to multiple subscribers across multiple protocols
Amazon SES for transactional email delivery
Amazon Pinpoint for targeted customer communication campaigns
Amazon SQS for reliable message delivery to queues
explain: Amazon SNS is a managed pub/sub messaging service that fans out messages to multiple subscribers simultaneously via email, SMS, HTTP, SQS queues, and Lambda functions, supporting millions of subscribers.

q: A company evaluates cloud resilience. Which architectural approach provides the highest reliability?
options: Single data center for simplicity
Backup-only approach
Manual failover between regions
Redundancy across multiple AZs with automated failover
explain: Deploying redundant resources across multiple Availability Zones with automated failover ensures high reliability: if one AZ fails, traffic automatically switches to healthy AZs.

q: Which AWS service provides managed Kubernetes for running containerized applications?
options: AWS Fargate for serverless container execution
AWS App Runner for fully managed containerized web apps
Amazon ECS for AWS-native container orchestration
Amazon EKS (Elastic Kubernetes Service) for managed Kubernetes clusters
explain: Amazon EKS manages the Kubernetes control plane (API server, etcd) across multiple AZs, handling upgrades and patching. You manage worker nodes (EC2 or Fargate) and deploy standard Kubernetes workloads.

q: A company needs to migrate a large amount of data (100TB) from on-premises to S3 but has limited internet bandwidth. Which service helps?
options: AWS DataSync for network-based transfer optimization
Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration for faster uploads
AWS Snowball Edge physical device for offline data migration
AWS Direct Connect providing dedicated 10Gbps connection
explain: AWS Snowball Edge is a physical device shipped to your location. You copy data to it and ship it back to AWS, which loads it into S3. For 100TB with limited bandwidth, this is faster and cheaper than network transfer.

q: Which AWS service provides dedicated, high-bandwidth private network connectivity between on-premises and AWS?
options: Amazon Route 53 for DNS-based routing to AWS
AWS VPN using encrypted tunnels over the internet
AWS Direct Connect providing dedicated private network connections
AWS Transit Gateway connecting VPCs and on-premises networks
explain: AWS Direct Connect establishes dedicated 1Gbps-100Gbps private network connections from on-premises to AWS, bypassing the public internet for consistent performance, lower latency, and reduced data transfer costs.

q: A company discusses AWS regions for their application deployment. Why would they deploy across multiple regions?
options: Single region reduces costs
All regions are identical so region choice doesn't matter
Regulations prohibit multi-region deployment
Multiple regions provide disaster recovery, low latency for global users, and regulatory compliance
explain: Multiple regions provide disaster recovery (if one region fails, others continue), low latency for global users (serve from nearest region), and compliance with data residency requirements.

q: A financial services company must comply with PCI DSS. Which AWS service provides compliance reports?
options: AWS Shield for DDoS protection
AWS Artifact for accessing compliance reports and certifications
AWS KMS for encryption
AWS GuardDuty for threat detection
explain: AWS Artifact provides on-demand access to AWS compliance reports (SOC reports, PCI DSS attestations, ISO certifications). Organizations use these to demonstrate compliance with regulatory requirements.

q: A company runs a critical application on EC2 and wants to ensure it continues running even if an entire AWS Availability Zone fails. What should they implement?
options: Deploy EC2 instances across multiple Availability Zones behind a load balancer
Enable EC2 instance recovery for automatic restart after failure
Use Reserved Instances for guaranteed instance availability
Use a larger EC2 instance type with more redundant hardware
explain: Deploying across multiple AZs with an Elastic Load Balancer ensures the application continues running if one AZ fails. The load balancer routes traffic only to healthy instances in operational AZs.

q: Which AWS service is a managed, serverless data integration service for discovering, preparing, and combining data for analytics?
options: AWS Glue for serverless ETL and data catalog
Amazon Kinesis for real-time streaming data ingestion
Amazon Redshift for data warehousing workloads
Amazon EMR for big data processing with Spark and Hadoop
explain: AWS Glue is a serverless ETL service with a Data Catalog that discovers schemas, crawls data sources, and transforms data for analytics. No servers to manage — pay only for compute used during job runs.

q: A DevOps engineer configures MFA for IAM users. Why is MFA critical for AWS security?
options: It encrypts all network traffic
It requires a second verification beyond the password, preventing account compromise if passwords leak
It automatically backs up user accounts
It eliminates the need for passwords
explain: MFA adds a second authentication factor (app, SMS, hardware key) beyond the password. Even if a password is compromised, attackers can't access the account without the second factor.

q: A company needs to protect their web application from SQL injection and XSS attacks. Which service should they use?
options: AWS KMS — managing encryption keys
AWS Shield — protecting against DDoS attacks
AWS WAF (Web Application Firewall) — filtering malicious requests
AWS GuardDuty — detecting intrusions
explain: AWS WAF protects web applications by filtering HTTP/HTTPS traffic based on rules. It blocks common attacks like SQL injection and XSS before they reach your application.

q: Which AWS service provides a managed Apache Kafka-compatible message streaming service?
options: Amazon MSK (Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka) for Kafka workloads
Amazon EventBridge for event-driven architecture
Amazon SQS for reliable message queue processing
Amazon Kinesis Data Streams for real-time streaming
explain: Amazon MSK (Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka) provisions and manages Kafka clusters, handling broker provisioning, patching, and availability. It's compatible with existing Kafka applications and tools.

q: A company wants to build a RESTful API for their mobile app with authentication, throttling, and caching. Which AWS service manages this?
options: AWS AppSync for GraphQL API management
Amazon API Gateway for RESTful and WebSocket API management
Elastic Load Balancer for HTTP request routing
AWS Lambda URL for direct function invocation via HTTP
explain: Amazon API Gateway creates, publishes, maintains, and secures RESTful and WebSocket APIs. It provides authentication (Cognito, Lambda authorizers), throttling, caching, request/response transformation, and SDK generation.

q: A healthcare organization must encrypt patient data at rest. What encryption method protects stored data?
options: Network encryption — protecting communication between data centers
SSL/TLS — protecting web browser connections
Encryption at rest — protecting stored data with cryptographic keys
Encryption in transit — protecting data while moving over networks
explain: Encryption at rest protects stored data (in S3, RDS, EBS) by encrypting it using AWS KMS keys. Even if someone physically accesses storage, data is unreadable without the encryption key.

q: Which AWS compute service is best for running scheduled batch jobs that process large datasets overnight?
options: Amazon ECS for containerized application workloads
Amazon EC2 with scheduled Auto Scaling policies
AWS Batch for fully managed batch computing workloads
AWS Lambda for event-driven serverless execution
explain: AWS Batch dynamically provisions optimal compute resources (EC2, Spot, Fargate) for batch jobs based on volume and requirements. It handles job scheduling, dependency management, and scales to hundreds of thousands of jobs.

q: A company wants to provide a shared file system accessible by multiple EC2 instances simultaneously across Availability Zones. Which service provides this?
options: Amazon FSx for Windows File Server for SMB protocol access
Amazon EBS volumes attached to single EC2 instances
Amazon EFS (Elastic File System) for shared NFS file storage across multiple AZs
Amazon S3 for object storage accessible from EC2 via API
explain: Amazon EFS is a managed NFS file system that automatically grows and shrinks, accessible simultaneously from multiple EC2 instances across multiple AZs. It's perfect for shared content repositories and CMS workloads.

q: A startup evaluates cloud maturity. What is the benefit of using managed AWS services over building infrastructure from scratch?
options: Managed services cost more
No operational benefits
Managed services are less reliable
Managed services handle operations (patches, backups, scaling), letting teams focus on applications
explain: Managed services (RDS, DynamoDB, Cognito) handle operations, backups, scaling, and maintenance. Teams focus on application logic, not infrastructure operations.

q: Which AWS service provides machine learning-powered search capabilities for websites and applications?
options: Amazon Comprehend for NLP text analysis
Amazon CloudSearch for managed search service
Amazon Elasticsearch Service for text search workloads
Amazon Kendra for intelligent ML-powered enterprise search
explain: Amazon Kendra uses ML to provide intelligent enterprise search, understanding natural language queries to find accurate answers from documents, FAQs, and knowledge bases rather than just keyword matching.

q: A company needs to analyze their existing workloads to identify which EC2 instances to right-size before migrating to AWS. Which service provides this?
options: AWS Compute Optimizer recommending optimal EC2 instance types
AWS Migration Hub for tracking migration progress
AWS Pricing Calculator estimating costs before migration
AWS Application Discovery Service collecting on-premises server data
explain: AWS Compute Optimizer uses ML to analyze historical utilization metrics and recommends optimal EC2 instance types, Auto Scaling groups, EBS volumes, and Lambda memory to reduce costs and improve performance.

q: A network administrator creates a Security Group allowing HTTP and HTTPS traffic. What does this provide?
options: It eliminates the need for network infrastructure
It acts as a stateful firewall controlling traffic at the instance level
It encrypts all traffic automatically
It replaces the need for encryption
explain: Security Groups are stateful firewalls controlling traffic to EC2 instances. Rules specify allowed inbound traffic; outbound responses automatically flow back.

q: A company transitioning to cloud asks: 'What replaces our on-premises data center?' Which AWS service is the foundation?
options: Amazon EC2 — virtual machines providing the compute foundation for cloud infrastructure
Lambda for all compute
CloudFront for all services
S3 for all infrastructure
explain: EC2 provides virtual machines (instances) that replicate the compute foundation of traditional data centers, but with auto-scaling and cloud benefits.

q: Which service provides automated deployment pipelines for continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD) on AWS?
options: AWS CloudFormation for infrastructure deployment automation
AWS Systems Manager for operational automation
AWS CodePipeline for orchestrating CI/CD workflows
AWS Elastic Beanstalk for application deployment management
explain: AWS CodePipeline orchestrates CI/CD pipelines connecting source (CodeCommit, GitHub), build (CodeBuild), and deploy (CodeDeploy, Elastic Beanstalk, ECS) stages for automated software release processes.

q: A company implements the principle of least privilege in IAM. Why is this security best practice critical?
options: It minimizes damage if credentials are compromised by limiting what the compromised account can do
It eliminates the need for firewalls
It simplifies administration
It has no security benefit
explain: Least privilege minimizes the blast radius of a compromise. If an attacker gains a user's credentials, they can only access resources the user needs, not the entire infrastructure.

q: A developer needs user authentication for a mobile app without building a login system. Which AWS service provides this?
options: AWS KMS for encryption key management
AWS Cognito for user authentication and identity management
AWS Shield for DDoS protection
AWS Artifact for compliance reports
explain: AWS Cognito provides user directories, authentication, sign-up, MFA, and social login. Developers integrate Cognito APIs without building authentication systems from scratch.

q: A company wants to quickly set up a virtual desktop for remote employees without managing physical desktops or VDI infrastructure. Which AWS service helps?
options: AWS Client VPN for secure remote network access
Amazon WorkSpaces for managed virtual desktop infrastructure
Amazon AppStream 2.0 for streaming individual applications
Amazon EC2 with Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP)
explain: Amazon WorkSpaces provides managed cloud desktops (Windows or Linux) accessible from anywhere. AWS manages the infrastructure, patching, and backup. Employees connect via the WorkSpaces client from any device.

q: Which AWS service enables running SQL queries directly on data stored in S3 without loading it into a database?
options: AWS Glue ETL for transforming S3 data before querying
Amazon Athena for serverless SQL queries directly on S3 data
Amazon Redshift Spectrum for S3 data querying
Amazon RDS with S3 integration for external tables
explain: Amazon Athena is a serverless interactive query service that runs standard SQL directly on data in S3 (CSV, JSON, Parquet, ORC) with no infrastructure to manage. Pay only per query based on data scanned.

q: A compliance officer wants visibility into AWS resource changes. Which service should they enable?
options: AWS Config — tracking resource configuration changes
AWS KMS — managing encryption
AWS GuardDuty — detecting malicious activity
AWS Shield — blocking DDoS attacks
explain: AWS Config continuously records AWS resource configuration changes, enabling compliance audits. This is essential for meeting regulatory requirements like SOC 2 and HIPAA.

q: An organization needs to detect anomalous account activity and potential intrusions. Which service is designed for this?
options: AWS KMS for encryption management
AWS WAF for application-layer protection
AWS Shield for DDoS protection
AWS GuardDuty for threat detection using machine learning
explain: AWS GuardDuty is a threat detection service using machine learning to analyze CloudTrail, DNS, and network logs, identifying potentially malicious activity and intrusions.

q: Which AWS service provides a globally distributed in-memory cache to reduce latency for DynamoDB tables?
options: AWS Global Accelerator routing DynamoDB requests globally
Amazon ElastiCache Redis in front of DynamoDB
Amazon CloudFront caching DynamoDB query results
Amazon DAX (DynamoDB Accelerator) providing microsecond read performance
explain: Amazon DAX is a fully managed, in-memory cache specifically for DynamoDB, providing microsecond response times for read-heavy workloads. It's API-compatible with DynamoDB, requiring minimal code changes.

q: Which AWS pricing model has no upfront costs and charges by the hour or second with no long-term commitments?
options: On-Demand pricing with pay-as-you-go billing
Spot Instances with variable pricing based on capacity
Dedicated Hosts with fixed monthly reservation fees
Reserved Instances with upfront payment commitment
explain: On-Demand pricing charges for compute, storage, and data transfer without upfront fees or minimum commitments. You pay only for what you use, making it ideal for unpredictable workloads and short-term experiments.

q: A company has multiple AWS accounts. How can they consolidate billing and receive volume discounts across all accounts?
options: Create a single AWS account and share access across teams
Contact AWS Sales to negotiate multi-account pricing
Enable AWS Cost Explorer across all accounts for unified billing
Use AWS Organizations with consolidated billing to combine usage across all accounts
explain: AWS Organizations consolidated billing aggregates usage across all linked accounts into a single bill. Combined usage may qualify for volume tier pricing discounts on services like S3 and data transfer.

q: A company needs to control encryption keys used for S3, RDS, and EBS. Which service should they use?
options: AWS Secrets Manager for managing database passwords
AWS KMS (Key Management Service) for creating and controlling encryption keys
IAM policies for managing keys
AWS CloudHSM for on-premises encryption
explain: AWS KMS manages encryption keys used by AWS services. It provides key creation, rotation, access control, and CloudTrail logging of all key usage.

q: Which AWS service allows you to set custom cost and usage budgets with alerts when spending exceeds thresholds?
options: AWS Budgets for proactive cost and usage alerts
AWS Pricing Calculator for estimating future costs
AWS Cost Explorer visualizing historical spending
AWS Cost and Usage Report for detailed billing data
explain: AWS Budgets lets you define budget thresholds for cost, usage, and Reserved Instance coverage. Alert notifications (email, SNS) trigger when actual or forecasted spending exceeds defined thresholds.

q: A compliance auditor requests logs of all API calls to AWS. Which service provides this audit trail?
options: AWS CloudWatch for monitoring metrics
AWS CloudTrail for logging API activity
AWS Config for resource configuration changes
AWS VPC Flow Logs for network traffic
explain: AWS CloudTrail records all API calls with details: who (user identity), when (timestamp), what (API action), and result. This is mandatory for compliance and security investigations.

q: A company notices a large unexpected AWS bill. Which service provides a detailed breakdown of costs by service, region, and tag?
options: AWS Cost Explorer for interactive cost visualization and analysis
AWS Cost and Usage Report for granular hourly billing data
AWS Trusted Advisor for cost optimization recommendations
AWS Budgets for budget threshold management
explain: AWS Cost Explorer provides interactive charts and graphs to analyze spending by service, region, linked account, and tags. It identifies cost trends, anomalies, and forecasts future spending based on historical patterns.

q: Which pricing model offers flexibility to change instance types within a family while still receiving discounts compared to On-Demand?
options: Standard Reserved Instances locked to a specific instance type
Convertible Reserved Instances allowing instance type changes within commitment
Compute Savings Plans for maximum flexibility across instance families
Spot Instances with dynamic pricing based on capacity
explain: Compute Savings Plans (not Convertible RIs) offer the most flexibility — they apply to any EC2 instance type, region, OS, and tenancy. Convertible RIs allow exchanges within the same family but require manual exchange requests.

q: A security team deploys resources in a VPC and uses Security Groups and NACLs. What layered security is this achieving?
options: Defense in depth — multiple security layers (network isolation + instance-level + subnet-level firewalls)
No security benefit from multiple layers
Only one of these is actually needed
Redundant and unnecessary security measures
explain: Combining VPC (network isolation), Security Groups (instance-level stateful firewall), and NACLs (subnet-level stateless firewall) implements defense in depth.

q: A company needs to enforce security compliance rules automatically. Which service helps enforce baselines?
options: AWS Secrets Manager for managing passwords
AWS CloudTrail for logging API calls
AWS Config for tracking configurations and enforcing compliance rules
AWS Trusted Advisor for cost optimization only
explain: AWS Config monitors resource configurations against custom rules and generates compliance reports. Non-compliant resources are flagged, enabling automated remediation.

q: A startup wants to reduce EC2 costs by committing to a consistent compute spend for 1 year. Which pricing option provides broad flexibility across instance types?
options: EC2 Reserved Instances for specific instance type discounts
Compute Savings Plans for flexible savings across any EC2 instance type
Dedicated Hosts for compliance and licensing optimization
EC2 Spot Instances for variable workload cost reduction
explain: Compute Savings Plans commit to a consistent hourly spend (e.g., $10/hr) for 1 or 3 years, applying discounts automatically to any EC2 instance type, family, region, OS, or tenancy — far more flexible than RIs.

q: Which AWS service provides a detailed, granular billing report with hourly or daily line items for every AWS service usage?
options: AWS Cost Explorer for interactive spending visualization
AWS Pricing Calculator for future cost estimation
AWS Cost and Usage Report (CUR) for comprehensive billing data
AWS Budgets for threshold-based cost monitoring
explain: AWS Cost and Usage Report delivers the most detailed billing data available — hourly or daily line items for every service, resource ID, pricing details, and cost allocation tags, delivered to S3 for custom analysis.

q: A company wants AWS to automatically alert them if their bill is forecasted to exceed $1,000 this month before they actually overspend. Which tool enables this?
options: AWS Budgets with forecasted cost alerts
AWS Trusted Advisor reviewing cost optimization opportunities
AWS Cost Explorer showing historical trends
AWS Pricing Calculator estimating costs upfront
explain: AWS Budgets can send alerts when forecasted costs are projected to exceed your budget threshold, giving you time to take action before the bill is finalized rather than after charges have been incurred.

q: What is the AWS Support plan that provides production system impaired response within 4 hours?
options: Basic Support — free tier with no production SLAs
Business Support — 24/7 phone/chat with 4-hour production impaired response
Developer Support — business hours email support only
Enterprise Support — 15-minute critical system down response
explain: AWS Business Support provides 24/7 phone/chat/email support with response times of: 4 hours for production system impaired, 1 hour for production system down, and 24 business hours for general guidance.

q: What AWS service provides recommendations to reduce EC2 costs by identifying idle and underutilized instances?
options: AWS Compute Optimizer right-sizing recommendations
AWS Cost Explorer Reserved Instance recommendations
AWS Config checking resource configuration compliance
AWS Trusted Advisor identifying cost optimization opportunities
explain: AWS Trusted Advisor checks for idle EC2 instances, unattached EBS volumes, and underutilized RDS instances. AWS Compute Optimizer complements this with ML-based right-sizing recommendations for optimal instance types.

q: A company purchases Reserved Instances for EC2 but their usage pattern changes and the RIs no longer align with their workload. What can they do?
options: Transfer Reserved Instances between AWS accounts for free
Contact AWS to cancel the Reserved Instances immediately
Sell unused Reserved Instances on the AWS Reserved Instance Marketplace
Convert Reserved Instances to On-Demand automatically
explain: The AWS Reserved Instance Marketplace allows customers to sell their unused Standard Reserved Instances to other AWS customers. This provides an exit option when workloads change and RIs no longer match usage.

q: Which AWS service enables you to view and manage all of your AWS costs from a single, integrated view even across multiple payer accounts?
options: AWS Cost Management console providing unified cost visibility
AWS Organizations for multi-account consolidated billing view
AWS Cost Explorer for a single account cost analysis
AWS Cost and Usage Report for detailed billing data export
explain: AWS Cost Management console (including Cost Explorer, Budgets, Cost and Usage Reports) provides unified visibility across all linked accounts in AWS Organizations, enabling cross-account cost analysis and optimization.

q: A company wants to enable encryption for data in transit (moving between EC2 and database). Which technology provides this?
options: No encryption for data in transit
SSL/TLS — encrypts data while moving over networks
Encryption at rest only
VPC isolation only
explain: SSL/TLS encrypts data while in transit. Combined with encryption at rest, this protects data in all states (moving and stored).

q: A company wants to track how much each team is spending on AWS. What is the best practice for cost attribution?
options: Ask teams to self-report their AWS resource usage monthly
Review monthly bills manually and estimate team costs
Use consistent cost allocation tags (e.g., team=engineering) on all resources and activate them in billing
Create separate AWS accounts for each team
explain: Consistent cost allocation tagging is the scalable best practice. Tag every resource with team, project, and environment tags, activate them in billing preferences, and filter Cost Explorer by tag to see per-team spending.

q: A company runs a web application on EC2 and experiences sudden traffic spike. How does Auto Scaling respond?
options: Auto Scaling automatically launches additional EC2 instances to handle the load
Manual intervention is required
Servers crash from overload
No solution exists for traffic spikes
explain: EC2 Auto Scaling Groups automatically launch instances when load increases (based on CloudWatch metrics) and terminate instances when load decreases.

q: Which AWS tool can help identify which EC2 Reserved Instances are underutilized and recommend purchasing options?
options: AWS Pricing Calculator for evaluating purchase options
AWS Cost Explorer with RI utilization and coverage reports
AWS Trusted Advisor checking RI utilization rates
AWS Budgets tracking RI coverage percentage
explain: AWS Cost Explorer provides Reserved Instance utilization reports (how much of your RI capacity was actually used) and coverage reports (what percentage of your usage was covered by RIs), plus purchase recommendations.

q: A company is choosing between AWS and building their own data center. Which total cost of ownership (TCO) factors favor AWS?
options: On-premises is always cheaper for steady-state workloads with predictable demand
AWS eliminates server hardware purchase, data center facility costs, power/cooling, and staff for infrastructure management
AWS pricing is transparent while on-premises has no hidden costs
AWS always costs less than on-premises for every workload
explain: AWS TCO advantages include: no capital expenditure on hardware, no facility costs, no power/cooling expenses, no hardware lifecycle management, and reduced staff for infrastructure. AWS TCO Calculator quantifies these savings.

q: AWS Trusted Advisor flags: 'security group allows unrestricted SSH access (0.0.0.0/0).' What risk does this represent?
options: High risk: anyone on the internet can attempt to connect via SSH
No risk; SSH is encrypted so it's always safe
Risk is eliminated if you use strong passwords
Risk only applies to production environments
explain: Allowing SSH from 0.0.0.0/0 exposes instances to brute force attacks and automated bot scanning. Best practice: restrict SSH to specific IPs or use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager.

q: Which AWS Support plan includes Infrastructure Event Management for major launches and migrations at no additional cost?
options: Enterprise On-Ramp for production workloads
Business Support included in standard pricing
Enterprise Support providing proactive engagement including IEM
Developer Support for growing companies
explain: Enterprise Support includes Infrastructure Event Management (IEM), where AWS Solution Architects provide hands-on operational guidance for significant events like product launches, migrations, and high-traffic events.

q: What are the 6 R's of cloud migration strategies? Which strategy involves rehosting an application without changes?
options: Replatform — making minor optimizations without changing core architecture
Refactor — redesigning the application to be cloud-native
Retire — decommissioning applications that are no longer needed
Rehost (lift-and-shift) — moving the application to cloud with no changes
explain: Rehost (lift-and-shift) moves applications to the cloud as-is with no code changes. It's the fastest migration approach, ideal for quickly getting out of a data center. Other Rs: Replatform, Refactor, Repurchase, Retire, Retain.

q: A company wants to replace their self-managed CRM with Salesforce on AWS. Which migration strategy is this?
options: Refactor — rebuilding the CRM as cloud-native microservices
Rehost — moving existing CRM to EC2 without changes
Repurchase — dropping the current app and switching to a different SaaS product
Replatform — upgrading the database engine during migration
explain: Repurchase (drop-and-shop) means abandoning the current application and purchasing a commercial off-the-shelf SaaS replacement like Salesforce, ServiceNow, or Workday. No migration of the old code needed.

q: A startup needs compute capacity with flexibility to scale. Which AWS service fits this?
options: Amazon RDS for databases only
AWS Lambda for serverless functions
Amazon EC2 for virtual machine instances with auto scaling
Amazon S3 for storage only
